Title: Flavones enrich rhizosphere Pseudomonas to enhance nitrogen utilization and secondary root growth in Populus

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Recruits beneficial rhizosphere Pseudomonas, enhancing biological nitrogen fixation contribution, nitrogen acquisition, plant growth, and secondary root development under low nitrogen
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive expression of PopGL3 increases tricin secretion, enriches rhizosphere Pseudomonas, increases biomass and leaf nitrogen accumulation in unsterilized low-nitrogen soil, and enhances root colonization by Pto1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression network analysis; DAP-seq; overexpression in 84K poplar; qRT-PCR of downstream genes; root/exudate metabolite profiling; rhizosphere 16S amplicon sequencing; RFP-labeled colonization assay; CFU quantification; biomass and leaf nitrogen phenotyping; 15N isotope dilution
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in Leuce poplar section under low nitrogen in natural soil
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in Leuce-associated/root samples; tested by overexpression in 84K roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-nitrogen soil; unsterilized versus sterilized soil; Pseudomonas colonization context
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome CRA015096; transgenic poplar amplicon data CRA015469; PopGL3 DAP-seq CRA015475; metabolomics figshare 10.6084/m9.figshare.26426578
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth advantage in sterilized soil; effect depends on microbiota context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although increased Pseudomonas abundance in transgenics may partly reflect depletion of other bacterial lineages, direct root colonization by Pto1 was also increased; acts as regulator of flavone biosynthesis with decisive role in tricin secretion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PopGL3 is a Leuce-enriched bHLH factor strongly co-expressed with flavones and flavonoid biosynthetic genes. 2. DAP-seq showed PopGL3 binds flavonoid-related genes including PopF3′H, PopPA2, PopDAHP, PopCCR1, and PopTHB. 3. PopGL3 overexpression activates PopF3′H and PopFNS, elevates tricin in roots/exudates, enriches Pseudomonas, and improves biomass and nitrogen accumulation in low-nitrogen unsterilized soil.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to enhance low-nitrogen adaptation by boosting tricin-mediated beneficial microbiome recruitment; suitable for stacking with pathway genes such as CHS4, but deployment should consider microbiome dependence.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry/committed step of the multi-branched flavonoid pathway leading to flavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form naringenin chalcone, the first committed intermediate of flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for tricin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step; key control point stated in paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports recruitment of beneficial Pseudomonas that enhance nitrogen acquisition and root growth under low nitrogen
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive expression of PopCHS4 increases tricin release, enriches rhizosphere Pseudomonas, enhances Pto1 colonization, and increases biomass and leaf nitrogen accumulation in unsterilized low-nitrogen soil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in 84K poplar; qRT-PCR of PopF3′H and PopFNS; root/exudate metabolite profiling; rhizosphere 16S amplicon sequencing; RFP-labeled colonization assay; CFU quantification; biomass and leaf nitrogen phenotyping; 15N isotope dilution
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in engineered lines; CHS family members enriched in Leuce-related Cluster IV, with four CHS genes in Cluster IV
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in 84K transgenic roots under low-nitrogen soil
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-nitrogen soil; unsterilized versus sterilized soil; beneficial microbiome context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome CRA015096; transgenic poplar amplicon data CRA015469; metabolomics figshare 10.6084/m9.figshare.26426578
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth advantage in sterilized soil; trait depends on soil microbiota
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes CHS as catalyzing the first committed step of the multi-branched flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PopCHS4 overexpression increases transcription of PopF3′H and PopFNS. 2. PopCHS4-OE plants secrete more tricin and reshape the rhizosphere to enrich Pseudomonas. 3. PopCHS4-OE plants show higher biomass, leaf nitrogen, and Pto1 colonization in unsterilized low-nitrogen soil.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway engineering target to increase flavone/tricin output and recruit beneficial microbiota for nitrogen-poor soils; can be stacked with PopGL3 for stronger effect.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates naringenin to eriodictyol and dihydrokaempferol to dihydroquercetin, generating key precursors for flavone and flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms key precursor for tricin/apigenin-related flavone branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-related step; described as pivotal for precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavones associated with recruitment of beneficial Pseudomonas and improved nitrogen utilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PopF3′H is directly regulated by PopGL3 and transcriptionally activated in PopGL3-OE and PopCHS4-OE lines, linking it to increased flavone/tricin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression network analysis; DAP-seq target identification by PopGL3; qRT-PCR in PopGL3-OE and PopCHS4-OE lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All three F3′H genes were present in Cluster IV enriched in Leuce
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated in PopGL3-OE and PopCHS4-OE poplar lines
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome CRA015096; PopGL3 DAP-seq CRA015475
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally manipulated alone in this paper, but experimentally implicated as a primary PopGL3 target within the causal flavone module
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PopF3′H genes are Leuce-enriched in the flavone-associated Cluster IV. 2. PopGL3 binds PopF3′H regulatory regions by DAP-seq. 3. PopF3′H transcript abundance increases in PopGL3-OE and PopCHS4-OE lines, consistent with elevated flavone/tricin pathway output.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway node for fine-tuning flux toward flavone precursors when combined with upstream or regulatory engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of flavanones into flavones in the flavone branch of flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms flavone products leading toward tricin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tricin promotes beneficial Pseudomonas behavior and supports nitrogen acquisition and root development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PopFNS transcription is activated in PopGL3-OE and PopCHS4-OE lines coinciding with higher tricin secretion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in transgenic lines; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in PopGL3-OE and PopCHS4-OE lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because transcriptional activation and metabolite outcomes place it in the core causal pathway, though not directly manipulated individually
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PopFNS is induced by PopGL3 or PopCHS4 constitutive expression. 2. Its activation parallels elevated tricin accumulation in roots and exudates. 3. This supports a role in the flavone branch driving microbiome-mediated nitrogen benefits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream enzyme target to strengthen flavone/tricin branch output in microbiome-oriented metabolic engineering.
